free_text: Energy and angular distributions of photoneutrons
The length of the X-ray pulse is about 200 microsec.
Angular distributions were approximated with a form
For the  heavy elements the angular distributions are
of  the  evaporation  process,  while  for the light
elements there  is  also  an  anisotropic  component
peaked at 90 degrees.  The energy distributions show
that most  of  neutrons  can  be  attributed  to  an
evaporation  process which is governed by a constant
A high-energy tail is attributed to direct interaction.
Data were compiled at SCJAD of Saratov
